html.q: Which tag is used for the largest heading?
html.explanation: The <h1> tag defines the largest and most important heading in HTML.
HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language.

html.q: Which element is used for line break?
html.explanation:
The
tag inserts a single line break.

html.q: Which attribute specifies an alternate text for an image?
html.explanation: The alt attribute provides alternative text for an image.

html.q: Which tag is used to create a hyperlink?
html.explanation: The <a> (anchor) tag is used to create hyperlinks.

html.q: Which tag is used to define an unordered list?
html.explanation: The <ul> tag defines an unordered (bulleted) list.

html.q: Which HTML attribute is used to define inline styles?
html.explanation: The style attribute is used to add inline CSS styles.

html.q: Which tag is used to define a table row?
html.explanation: The <tr> tag defines a row in an HTML table.

html.q: Which input type defines a slider control?
html.explanation: The input type 'range' creates a slider control.

html.q: Which element is used for the document's body?
html.explanation: The <body> element contains all the visible page content.

html.q: Which tag creates a dropdown list?
html.explanation: The <select> tag creates a dropdown list.
The <em> tag emphasizes text, typically rendering it in italic.

html.q: Which attribute is used to specify the URL of an image?
html.explanation: The src attribute specifies the path to the image.

css.q: Which property is used to change the background color?
css.explanation: The background-color property sets the background color of an element.

css.q: Which CSS property controls the text size?
css.explanation: The font-size property sets the size of the text.

css.q: How do you select an element with id 'demo'?
css.explanation: The # selector is used to select elements by their id.

css.q: Which property is used to change the font?
css.explanation: The font-family property specifies the font for text.

css.q: How do you make text bold?
css.explanation: font-weight: bold makes text appear bold.

css.q: Which property adds space between elements' border and content?
css.explanation: Padding adds space between an element's content and its border.

css.q: Which value of display makes an element invisible but keeps its space?
css.explanation: visibility: hidden hides the element while keeping its space.
border-radius creates rounded corners on elements.

css.q: Which unit is relative to the font-size of the element?
css.explanation: The em unit is relative to the font-size of the element.

css.q: What is the default position of HTML elements?
css.explanation: By default, HTML elements have position: static.

css.q: Which property controls the order of overlapping elements?
css.explanation: z-index controls the stacking order of overlapping elements.

css.q: Which CSS property is used for flexbox layout?
css.explanation: display: flex enables flexbox layout on a container.
The opacity property sets the transparency level (0 to 1).

js.q: Which keyword is used to define a constant?
js.explanation: The const keyword declares a block-scoped constant.

js.q: What is the output of typeof null?
js.explanation: typeof null returns 'object' — a well-known JavaScript quirk.

js.q: Which method adds an element to the end of an array?
js.explanation: The push() method adds elements to the end of an array.
The === operator checks both value and type equality.

js.q: Which function parses a string to an integer?
js.explanation: parseInt() parses a string and returns an integer.

js.q: What is the correct way to write an arrow function?

js.explanation: JSON stands for JavaScript Object Notation.

js.q: Which loop is best for iterating over object properties?
js.explanation: The for...in loop iterates over the enumerable properties of an object.

js.q: What is the purpose of 'use strict'?
js.explanation: 'use strict' enables strict mode for catching common errors.

js.q: What does the spread operator look like?
js.explanation: The spread operator uses three dots (...).

o: A function with access to its outer scope
js.explanation: A closure is a function that has access to variables from its outer scope.

js.q: Which method creates a new array with transformed elements?
js.explanation: The map() method creates a new array with the results of calling a function on every element.

js.q: What is the purpose of Promise in JavaScript?
js.explanation: Promises handle asynchronous operations and their results.

py.q: Which keyword is used to define a function in Python?
py.explanation: The 'def' keyword is used to define functions in Python.

py.q: What is the output of print(type([]))?
py.explanation: [] creates a list in Python, so type([]) returns <class 'list'>.

py.q: Which method adds an element to a list?
py.explanation: The append() method adds an element to the end of a list.

py.q: How do you start a comment in Python?
py.explanation: Comments in Python start with the # symbol.

py.q: Which operator is used for exponentiation?
py.explanation: The ** operator performs exponentiation in Python.

py.q: Which function returns the length of a list?
py.explanation: The len() function returns the number of items in a list.

py.q: Which keyword is used for exception handling?
py.explanation: Python uses 'except' blocks for exception handling.

py.q: What does the 'self' parameter refer to?
py.explanation: 'self' refers to the current instance of the class.

o: A way to create lists concisely
py.explanation: List comprehensions provide a concise way to create lists.
The 'pass' statement is a null operation used as a placeholder.
Tuples are immutable — their elements cannot be changed after creation.
Lambda functions are small anonymous functions defined with the lambda keyword.

c.q: Which function is used to print output in C?
c.explanation: printf() is the standard output function in C.

c.q: What is the correct syntax for the main function?
c.explanation: int main() is the standard entry point for a C program.

c.q: Which header file is needed for printf?
Which operator is used to access a structure member?
c.explanation: The . operator accesses members directly, while -> accesses through a pointer.

c.q: What is the size of int on most systems?
c.explanation: On most modern systems, an int is 4 bytes (32 bits).
malloc() returns a void pointer (void*) to allocated memory.

c.q: Which loop is guaranteed to execute at least once?
c.explanation: The do-while loop always executes its body at least once.
A pointer is a variable that stores the memory address of another variable.
The & operator returns the memory address of a variable.

c.q: What is the null character in C?
c.explanation: \0 is the null character that terminates strings in C.
